rationale: A0 shows schtasks.exe creating a scheduled task named 'SoundIentifier' (deliberate typo to evade detection) that executes a payload via WMIC process creation targeting an Alternate Data Stream (ADS) path 'C:\Users\Public\Disk101.ini:59e9c935' — a classic ADS-based payload hiding technique. EV-001 shows a second scheduled task 'Disk Diag' created within one second, configured to run every 6 minutes executing 'C:\Users\Public\Weber0.js' via explorer.exe, establishing persistence via a suspicious JS file in the public directory. Both tasks are created from the same cmd.exe parent (PID 1592) under corp\Administrator at High integrity, indicating a scripted/automated attack sequence. The combination of ADS-based payload execution, recurring scheduled task persistence, use of public directories for payloads, and deliberate task name obfuscation are strong indicators of post-exploitation activity.
